Acceptance criteria: all 23 crates described with enough specificity for onboarding; descriptions do not contradict dependency layers in §8.1-§8.2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:38.969878831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:57.554521957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:57.554471814Z","close_reason":"Completed §8.3 compliance implementation: unit/e2e identifiers wired plus deterministic e2e doc-compliance runner with structured logging","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","depends_on_id":"bd-1wwc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","depends_on_id":"bd-3an","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1998,"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §8.3 Per-Crate Detailed Descriptions

### fsqlite-types (~3,500 LOC)
Foundational types, zero internal deps. Modules: page.rs (PageNumber/NonZeroU32, PageBuf/PageData page-aligned, PageSize), value.rs (SqliteValue enum), opcode.rs (190+ Opcodes + OpcodeInfo), serial.rs (SerialType encoding/decoding), record.rs (Record struct, header, ser/de), txn.rs (TxnId u64, TxnMode enum), flags.rs (OpenFlags, SyncFlags, AccessFlags, LockLevel), limits.rs (SQLITE_MAX_*), affinity.rs (TypeAffinity), collation.rs (CollationId, BINARY/NOCASE/RTRIM). ~80 types, all Debug+Clone, most Copy.

### fsqlite-error (~800 LOC)
Error types via thiserror. FrankenError enum (~40 variants), ErrorCode (~30 primary), extended codes (SQLITE_BUSY_RECOVERY etc.), Result type alias. Every variant carries context (operation, page/table, source error).

### fsqlite-vfs (~2,500 LOC)
VFS abstraction = sqlite3_vfs + sqlite3_io_methods. traits.rs (Vfs/VfsFile), memory.rs (MemoryVfs with HashMap<PathBuf, Arc<Mutex<Vec<u8>>>>), unix.rs (UnixVfs via asupersync blocking I/O, fcntl F_SETLK, 5 lock levels), flags.rs (VfsOpenFlags). Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, asupersync.

### fsqlite-pager (~4,000 LOC)
Page cache + txn state machine. pager.rs (state: Open->Reader->Writer->Error, manages db file + journal + ARC cache, defines MvccPager + CheckpointPageWriter traits), cache.rs (ArcCache impl S6), page_ref.rs (RAII PageRef, decrements ref_count on drop), journal.rs (rollback journal, hot journal detection), state.rs (PagerState enum), header.rs (100-byte db header). Deps: fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error.

### fsqlite-wal (~3,500 LOC)
WAL implementation. wal.rs (Wal struct, header parsing, frame append, cumulative checksum S7.1), frame.rs (WalFrame 24B header + page data), index.rs (WalIndex SHM hash table, linear probing, reader marks, lock bytes), checkpoint.rs (PASSIVE/FULL/RESTART/TRUNCATE), recovery.rs (checksum chain validation, committed txn replay, RaptorQ self-healing), raptorq.rs (repair symbol gen on commit, decode during recovery). Deps: fsqlite-vfs, asupersync. Does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager (cycle broken via CheckpointPageWriter trait).

### fsqlite-mvcc (~3,000 LOC)
MVCC version management — heart of concurrency innovation. manager.rs (MvccManager: txns, version store, lock table, commit index, witness hooks, GC), snapshot.rs (Snapshot{high, schema_epoch}, capture, visibility predicate), version.rs (PageVersion, arena-backed version chains), lock_table.rs (InProcessPageLockTable sharded HashMap + ShmPageLockTable adapter), transaction.rs (Transaction lifecycle Active->Committed/Aborted, write set, intent log, witness keys), commit.rs (FCW via CommitIndex + merge ladder, coordinator publication), gc.rs (horizon, chain pruning, reclaimability), coordinator.rs (WriteCoordinator wrapping asupersync two-phase MPSC). Deps: fsqlite-wal, fsqlite-pager, parking_lot, asupersync.

### fsqlite-btree (~5,000 LOC)
B-tree storage engine, most complex after VDBE. cursor.rs (BtCursor, page stack max depth 20@4KB/40@512B, save/restore), cell.rs (IntKeyCell, BlobKeyCell, InteriorCell, varint), balance.rs (balance_nonroot redistribution, balance_deeper new root, balance_quick fast append), overflow.rs (overflow page chains), free_list.rs (trunk/leaf, allocate/grow/deallocate), payload.rs (BtreePayload spanning local+overflow), table.rs (table B-tree intkey ops), index.rs (index B-tree blobkey ops). Deps: fsqlite-pager (MvccPager trait), fsqlite-types.

### fsqlite-ast (~2,000 LOC)
SQL AST nodes. stmt.rs (~20 Statement variants), expr.rs (~30 Expr variants), select.rs (SelectStatement, SelectCore enum with Select{}/Values(), CompoundOp, JoinClause, OrderingTerm, LimitClause, WithClause, Cte), table_ref.rs (TableRef enum), ddl.rs (ColumnDef, TableConstraint, IndexedColumn, ForeignKeyClause), literal.rs (Literal enum incl CurrentTime/Date/Timestamp), operator.rs (BinaryOp, UnaryOp), span.rs (Span byte offset range). All nodes carry Span.

### fsqlite-parser (~4,500 LOC)
Lexer + recursive descent. lexer.rs (~150 TokenType variants, memchr-accelerated, line/column tracking), parser.rs (one method per production, Pratt precedence for expressions), keyword.rs (perfect hash for 150+ keywords via phf), error.rs (parse errors with span, expected tokens, recovery hints).

### fsqlite-planner (~3,000 LOC)
Query planning. resolve.rs (name resolution, alias binding, column refs, star expansion, subquery scoping), where_clause.rs (index-usable terms, range constraints, OR optimization), join.rs (join ordering, beam search best-first mxChoice=12/18 matching wherePathSolver), cost.rs (I/O cost per access path, selectivity from sqlite_stat1/stat4), index.rs (usability, covering detection), plan.rs (QueryPlan output).

### fsqlite-vdbe (~6,000 LOC)
Bytecode VM, largest crate. vm.rs (fetch-execute loop, match dispatch, PC management), mem.rs (Mem/sqlite3_value, multi-representation, affinity, comparison), cursor.rs (VdbeCursor wrapping BtCursor, deferred seek, cached row decode, pseudo-table), program.rs (VdbeProgram Vec<VdbeOp>, register allocation, coroutine state), op.rs (VdbeOp struct, P4 enum), sort.rs (external merge sort), compare.rs (record comparison with collation), func_dispatch.rs (scalar/aggregate/window dispatch), subtype.rs (JSON subtype management).

### fsqlite-func (~2,500 LOC)
~80 built-in functions. scalar.rs (~60: abs, char, hex, instr, length, lower...), aggregate.rs (~12: avg, count, sum, group_concat...), window.rs (~11: row_number, rank, lag, lead...), math.rs (acos, sin, sqrt, log...), info.rs (sqlite_version, changes, total_changes, last_insert_rowid), registry.rs (FunctionRegistry: (name, arg_count) -> impl).

### Extensions
- fsqlite-ext-json (~2,000 LOC): JSON1, json(), json_extract/set/remove/type/valid, json_each/tree vtabs, JSONB, ->/-->>
- fsqlite-ext-fts5 (~4,000 LOC): FTS5, Porter stemmer, unicode61, inverted index, BM25, highlight/snippet, custom tokenizer API
- fsqlite-ext-fts3 (~2,000 LOC): FTS3/4 compat, matchinfo/offsets/snippet, wraps FTS5
- fsqlite-ext-rtree (~2,000 LOC): R*-tree spatial index, nearest-neighbor, geopoly
- fsqlite-ext-session (~1,500 LOC): Changeset/patchset gen/apply/invert
- fsqlite-ext-icu (~800 LOC): ICU collation, unicode comparison, case folding, FTS tokenizer
- fsqlite-ext-misc (~1,500 LOC): generate_series, dbstat, dbpage, csv vtab, decimal, uuid, ieee754, carray

### fsqlite-core (~5,000 LOC)
Orchestration layer. connection.rs (Connection, open/close, ATTACH/DETACH, schema cache, auto-commit, busy handler, auth callback), prepare.rs (SQL pipeline: parse->resolve->plan->codegen, LRU statement cache), schema.rs (sqlite_master loading, Table/Index/View/Trigger objects, schema cookie), codegen.rs (AST->VDBE for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, expression codegen, subquery/CTE coroutines), pragma.rs (~80 pragmas), auth.rs (authorization dispatch), vtab.rs (virtual table registration/lifecycle).

### fsqlite (~1,000 LOC)
Public API facade. Database wraps Connection. Re-exports Statement, Row, Transaction, SqliteValue, PageNumber, FrankenError, ErrorCode, Result, Vfs, VfsFile, MemoryVfs. Convenience: open(), open_in_memory(), execute(cx, sql).await, query_row(cx, sql).await.

### fsqlite-cli (~2,000 LOC)
Interactive shell via frankentui. Dot-commands (.tables, .schema, .mode, .import, .dump, .headers, .separator). Output modes (column, csv, json, line, list, table). Tab completion, syntax highlighting, history.

### fsqlite-harness (~1,500 LOC)
Conformance test runner. Runs identical SQL against FrankenSQLite + C sqlite3. Row-by-row comparison. Error code matching. Golden file management.

1. **test_every_workspace_crate_has_description**: Verify that every workspace member under `crates/` has a corresponding subsection in the §8.3 crate descriptions.
2. **test_description_includes_purpose_and_key_modules**: For each crate description, ensure it names:
   - its purpose
   - 3-10 key modules/files
   - the direction of dependencies (what it depends on, and what depends on it)

## E2E Verification

- **e2e/onboarding_walkthrough.md** (planned or existing doc): follow a “cold start” path:
  - pick 3 representative tasks (storage, SQL pipeline, MVCC)
  - confirm that a developer can locate the correct crate(s) and entrypoints using only the §8.3 descriptions + beads.

## Logging Requirements

- Any automated doc-check should print a deterministic report:
  - missing crates, duplicated crates, and sections lacking module lists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 23 crates are described with enough specificity to serve as an onboarding map.
- The descriptions do not contradict the dependency layers in §8.1-§8.2.

 {"id":"bd-t6sv2","title":"MVCC Observability, Guidance & Differentiation Program","description":"Comprehensive program to make FrankenSQLite's concurrent writer capabilities observable, debuggable, and demonstrably superior. This epic consolidates 15 improvement ideas generated via idea-wizard methodology.

**PHASE 1: Core Observability (P1) — Foundation Layer**
┌────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     ID     │                      Feature                      │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.1 │ MVCC Conflict Analytics & Observability Suite     │
│            │ FOUNDATION: shared observability infra + conflict  │
│            │ analytics. ALL Phase 2 beads depend on this.       │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.2 │ Concurrent Writer Benchmark Suite & Regression CI │
│            │ Depends on .1 for conflict correlation             │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.3 │ RaptorQ Self-Healing Observability & Verification │
│            │ Depends on .1 for shared observability infra       │
└────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

**PHASE 2: User Guidance (P2) — Observability Consumers**
┌────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     ID     │                   Feature                        │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.4 │ SQLite Conformance & Compatibility Dashboard     │
│            │ Independent. MVCC divergence focus only.           │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.5 │ Transaction Lifecycle Analyzer & Advisor         │
│            │ Depends on .1. Feeds .7, .10, .15.                │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.6 │ Query Plan Concurrency Hints                     │
│            │ Depends on .1.                                    │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.7 │ Checkpoint Scheduling Advisor                    │
│            │ Depends on .5.                                    │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.8 │ Page Cache Efficiency Monitor                    │
│            │ Depends on .1. Feeds .9, .11, .15.                │
├────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ bd-t6sv2.9 │ Write Amplification Tracker                      │
│            │ Depends on .8. Feeds .11.                         │
└────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

**PHASE 3: Best Practices (P2-P3) — Analysis & Guidance**

 {"id":"bd-t6sv2.1","title":"MVCC Conflict Analytics & Observability Suite","description":"**Idea #1 - Top Priority**

Build comprehensive conflict observability into the MVCC layer, extending the existing `tracing` instrumentation (70+ usages across 18 MVCC source files).

**Components:**
1. **ConflictTracer** - Extend fsqlite-mvcc conflict instrumentation
   - Page lock contention: which txn, which page, resolution (extend `shared_lock_table.rs`, `conflict_model.rs`)
   - FCW (First Committer Wins) activations with before/after state (extend `conflict_model.rs`)
   - SSI serialization failures with dependency graph (extend `ssi_validation.rs`)
   - Zero-cost path when tracing disabled (compile-time feature gate)

2. **ConflictMetrics** - Aggregated statistics via connection pragma
   - PRAGMA fsqlite_conflict_stats: conflicts_total, conflicts_by_page, avg_resolution_time
   - Per-table conflict hotspot detection
   - Temporal conflict density (conflicts/second)

3. **ConflictLog** - Persistent conflict history for debugging
   - Ring buffer of last N conflicts (configurable via PRAGMA)
   - Export to JSON for external analysis
   - Integration with deterministic replay (bd-t6sv2.12)

4. **Shared Observability Infrastructure** - Foundation for beads 2, 3, 5, 8, 9
   - NEW: crates/fsqlite-observability/ with metrics registry, ring buffer, PRAGMA helpers
   - Trait-based observer pattern for zero-overhead opt-in
   - Structured event schema shared across all observability features

**Implementation:**
- Extend `conflict_observer` callback pattern in InProcessPageLockTable (`shared_lock_table.rs`)
- Extend Transaction struct in `lifecycle.rs` with conflict_history vec
- NEW: crates/fsqlite-observability/src/lib.rs (shared cross-cutting infrastructure)
- Build on existing `tracing` spans already in `core_types.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, `gc.rs`
- Proptest: verify conflict emission matches actual contention

**Files to Modify:**
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/shared_lock_table.rs (lock contention events)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/conflict_model.rs (FCW/SSI events)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/ssi_validation.rs (SSI failure detail)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs (transaction-level conflict history)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/pragma.rs (new PRAGMAs)
- NEW: crates/fsqlite-observability/src/lib.rs (shared infrastructure)

**CRITICAL: MVCC Preservation**
This feature ONLY observes conflicts - it MUST NOT introduce any serialization or blocking. Conflict tracing is purely diagnostic. The observer callback must be non-blocking and must not acquire any page locks.

**Test Requirements:**
- Unit: conflict event emission correctness with tracing enabled
  - Log: event type, txn_id, page_id, timestamp_ns, resolution outcome
  - Verify: zero events emitted when tracing disabled (feature gate test)
  - Verify: overhead < 1% when tracing disabled via criterion micro-benchmark
- Unit: ring buffer capacity and eviction behavior
  - Log: buffer size, oldest event age, eviction count
- Integration: multi-writer scenarios produce expected conflict events
  - Log: full conflict chain with page-level detail, txn lifecycle
  - Scenario: 4 writers hitting same page, verify exactly 3 FCW events
  - Scenario: SSI write-skew detection emits rw-antidependency edges
- E2E: PRAGMA returns accurate stats after conflict workload
  - Log: before/after stats, query execution time, conflicts/second
  - Verify: stats reset via PRAGMA fsqlite_conflict_reset
  - Verify: stats survive connection close/reopen within same process
- Proptest: conflict events match actual lock contention outcomes
  - Log: seed values for reproducibility, operation sequence
  - Property: every lock contention produces exactly one conflict event
  - Property: conflict_count == sum(conflicts_by_page)

**Verification Script:**

2. Captures conflict events via PRAGMA fsqlite_conflict_log
3. Verifies event count matches expected (with tolerance for timing)
4. Verifies each event has complete metadata (txn_id, page, resolution)
5. Outputs pass/fail with detailed diff and JSON evidence

**Success Criteria:**
- Developers can trace exactly why a transaction failed
- Hotspot detection identifies tables needing schema redesign
- Stats reset via PRAGMA fsqlite_conflict_reset
- Zero overhead when observability disabled

-{"id":"bd-t6sv2.10","title":"Connection Pooling Best Practices & Validator","description":"**Idea #10 - Phase 3**

Guide users toward optimal connection management for concurrency. This bead is primarily guidance and validation tooling, building on transaction lifecycle data from bd-t6sv2.5.

**Components:**
1. **PoolPatternDetector**
   - Detect anti-patterns: single connection for concurrent use (serialized writes)
   - Identify over-pooling: more connections than useful (too many snapshots block GC)
   - Find connection leaks: long-idle connections holding snapshots
   - Detect rapid connect/disconnect cycling (connection thrashing)

2. **PoolAdvisor**
   - Recommend pool size based on workload: min(CPU_cores, write_concurrency_needed)
   - Suggest connection lifecycle settings (max idle time, max age)
   - Warn about statement preparation patterns (unprepared queries in hot loops)
   - Use transaction lifecycle data from bd-t6sv2.5 for recommendations

3. **BestPracticesDoc**
   - Connection pooling guide specific to FrankenSQLite MVCC model
   - Key insight: unlike C SQLite, FrankenSQLite benefits from multiple writer connections
   - Comparison with C SQLite patterns (single-writer pool vs MVCC multi-writer pool)
   - Integration examples for common Rust pool libraries (sqlx, r2d2, deadpool, bb8)

4. **PoolSimulator**
   - Test harness for pool configurations
   - Model concurrent connection behavior under various pool sizes
   - Find optimal pool size empirically for given workload
   - Uses benchmark infrastructure from bd-t6sv2.2

**Implementation:**
- Connection-level metrics in `fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` (connect/disconnect tracking, idle time)
- Analysis utilities in fsqlite-observability (pool pattern analysis)
- PRAGMA fsqlite_connection_stats: pool_size_estimate, idle_connections, connection_age_max_ms
- Documentation in docs/connection-pooling.md

**Files to Modify:**
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (connection lifecycle tracking)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/pragma.rs (connection stats PRAGMA)
- crates/fsqlite-observability/ (pool pattern detection)
- docs/connection-pooling.md (comprehensive guide)

**CRITICAL: MVCC Preservation**
Pool recommendations MUST emphasize that FrankenSQLite benefits from multiple writer connections (unlike C SQLite). Never suggest a single-writer pool as a "safe default" - that defeats the entire purpose of MVCC. Connection tracking MUST NOT introduce connection-level serialization.

**Test Requirements:**
- Unit: anti-pattern detection accuracy
  - Log: pattern_type (single_conn|over_pool|conn_leak|conn_thrash), confidence_score, evidence
  - Verify: single-connection-for-concurrent-use correctly detected
  - Verify: no false positives for healthy pool configurations
- Unit: pool size recommendation calculation
  - Log: workload_profile (read_heavy|write_heavy|mixed), recommended_pool_size, rationale
  - Verify: recommendation increases with write concurrency need
  - Verify: recommendation bounded by diminishing returns threshold
- Unit: connection lifecycle tracking
  - Log: conn_id, connect_ts, idle_since_ts, queries_executed, txn_count
  - Verify: lifecycle tracking accurate under concurrent connection use
- Integration: pool pattern detection under simulated workloads
  - Log: pool_size, concurrent_writers, conflicts_detected, recommendation
  - Scenario: single connection with 4 threads -> detect anti-pattern
  - Scenario: 100 connections with 4 threads -> detect over-pooling
  - Scenario: connections never closed -> detect leak pattern
- Integration: advisor recommendations improve throughput
  - Log: pool_size_before, throughput_before, pool_size_after, throughput_after, improvement_pct
  - Verify: following pool size recommendation improves throughput
- E2E: documentation examples execute correctly
  - Log: example_name, execution_result (pass|fail), error_if_any
  - Verify: each code example in docs compiles and produces expected output
  - Verify: pool configuration examples demonstrate correct behavior
- E2E: pool simulator produces consistent recommendations
  - Log: workload_params, simulated_throughput, recommended_config, confidence
  - Verify: simulator recommendations stable across runs (within noise)

**Verification Script:**
Create scripts/verify_pool_advisor.sh that:
1. Creates pools of various sizes with concurrent workload
2. Runs anti-pattern detection on each configuration
3. Verifies advisor recommendations match expected patterns
4. Tests documentation examples for correctness
5. Outputs pass/fail with recommendation accuracy stats

**Success Criteria:**
- Users configure pools correctly for MVCC on first attempt
- Anti-patterns detected and explained with MVCC-specific context
- Documentation covers common scenarios with executable examples
- Pool recommendations backed by empirical simulator data

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-t6sv2.11","title":"Schema Design Advisor for Concurrency","description":"**Idea #11 - Phase 3**

Analyze schema design for concurrent write performance. Consumes conflict analytics from bd-t6sv2.1 and page cache data from bd-t6sv2.8 to provide data-driven schema recommendations.

**Components:**
1. **HotspotDetector**
   - Identify tables with single-row contention patterns (auto-increment rowid on last page)
   - Find auto-increment columns causing page contention (all INSERTs hit the rightmost leaf)
   - Detect wide rows that dominate pages (few rows per page = more conflicts per row)
   - Use conflict analytics from bd-t6sv2.1 to identify actual hotspot pages

2. **IndexAnalyzer**
   - Evaluate index selectivity for lock scope (more selective = fewer pages locked)
   - Identify missing indexes that cause table scans (scan locks all pages)
   - Find redundant indexes adding write overhead (each index is a separate B-tree)
   - Quantify write amplification contribution per index (using data from bd-t6sv2.9)

3. **SchemaAdvisor**
   - Suggest WITHOUT ROWID tables for UUID-keyed tables (spreads inserts across pages)
   - Recommend covering indexes for read-heavy queries (avoid table page access)
   - Propose column ordering to minimize page splits (fixed-width columns first)
   - Identify tables where page size adjustment would reduce conflicts

4. **SchemaSimulator**
   - Model lock contention for given schema + workload profile
   - Compare alternatives quantitatively (estimated conflicts/second)
   - Generate ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX migration statements
   - Uses page cache data from bd-t6sv2.8 for cache impact estimation

**Implementation:**
- Schema analysis utilities in `fsqlite-planner` (index selectivity, page density estimation)
- Integration with conflict statistics from bd-t6sv2.1
- PRAGMA fsqlite_schema_analysis: run analysis on demand, output JSON report
- Documentation and examples in docs/schema-concurrency.md

**Files to Modify:**
- crates/fsqlite-planner/src/ (schema analysis, index selectivity estimation)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/pragma.rs (PRAGMA fsqlite_schema_analysis)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (schema analysis registration)
- crates/fsqlite-observability/ (consume conflict stats from bd-t6sv2.1)
- docs/schema-concurrency.md (guide with examples)

**CRITICAL: MVCC Preservation**
Schema analysis is purely advisory and read-only. It MUST NOT alter any schema, data, or concurrency settings. Recommendations must always assume concurrent_mode=true. Never suggest schema changes that would reduce MVCC effectiveness.

**Test Requirements:**
- Unit: hotspot detection accuracy
  - Log: table_name, hotspot_type (autoincrement|wide_row|single_page), confidence, page_id
  - Verify: auto-increment table correctly identified as rightmost-leaf hotspot
  - Verify: wide-row table identified when avg_rows_per_page < 5
  - Verify: no false positives for well-distributed tables
- Unit: index analysis correctness
  - Log: index_name, selectivity, estimated_lock_scope_pages, redundant_with, write_amplification_contribution
  - Verify: higher selectivity indexes recommended over lower
  - Verify: redundant index detection (prefix of another index)
- Unit: advisor recommendation quality
  - Log: recommendation_type, table, rationale, estimated_improvement_pct, migration_sql
  - Verify: recommendations are valid SQL
  - Verify: estimated improvement correlates with actual (within 50%)
- Integration: schema simulator accuracy
  - Log: schema_description, simulated_conflicts_per_sec, actual_conflicts_per_sec, error_pct
  - Scenario: high-contention auto-increment table -> simulate, then verify vs actual
  - Scenario: apply recommendation -> verify improvement matches simulation
- Integration: full analysis pipeline
  - Log: tables_analyzed, hotspots_found, recommendations_count, analysis_time_ms
  - Verify: analysis completes in reasonable time (< 5s for 50-table schema)
  - Verify: recommendations reference actual conflict data from bd-t6sv2.1
- E2E: recommendations actually improve concurrency performance
  - Log: schema_before, recommendation, schema_after, throughput_before, throughput_after, conflict_rate_before, conflict_rate_after
  - Verify: at least one recommendation produces measurable improvement
  - Verify: no recommendation degrades performance
- E2E: documentation examples executable
  - Log: example_name, tables_created, analysis_run, recommendation_applied, result
  - Verify: each example in docs produces expected analysis output

**Verification Script:**
Create scripts/verify_schema_advisor.sh that:
1. Creates tables with known hotspot patterns
2. Runs schema analysis and captures recommendations
3. Applies recommendations and measures improvement
4. Verifies all recommendation SQL is valid
5. Outputs pass/fail with before/after comparison

**Success Criteria:**
- Schema problems identified before deployment with concrete data
- Concurrency-optimal design patterns documented with examples
- Migration path from problematic schemas with generated SQL
- Recommendations backed by actual conflict data (not just heuristics)

 {"id":"bd-t6sv2.12","title":"Deterministic Replay Debugger","description":"**Idea #12 - Phase 3**

Enable reproducible debugging of concurrent write scenarios. Build on existing infrastructure: `repro_fcw_loss.rs` for FCW reproduction, `deterministic_rebase.rs` for rebase replay, `time_travel.rs` for time-travel queries, and asupersync's Lab reactor for deterministic scheduling.

**Components:**
1. **RecordMode**
   - Capture all inputs: queries, timing, page state, random seeds
   - Deterministic timestamp assignment (logical clock, not wall clock)
   - Compact binary format for recording (versioned for forward compatibility)
   - Record MVCC version chain state at conflict points
   - Leverage asupersync's Lab reactor for deterministic scheduling capture

2. **ReplayMode**
   - Reproduce exact same execution order using captured schedule
   - Step through conflicts frame-by-frame with inspector hooks
   - Verify behavior matches recording (state assertion checkpoints)
   - Support concurrent_mode=true replays (CRITICAL - never serialize for replay)
   - Use DPOR-based schedule replay from asupersync

3. **MinimalReproducer**
   - Automatically minimize failure case (delta debugging)
   - Extract relevant transactions only (dependency-based slicing)
   - Generate standalone test case (compilable Rust test or SQL script)
   - Output includes: minimal schema, minimal data, minimal operations

4. **DiffAnalyzer**
   - Compare two executions (expected vs actual)
   - Identify divergence point (first differing MVCC version chain state)
   - Explain behavioral differences at page-level detail
   - Highlight MVCC state divergence (version chain, lock table, SSI graph)

**Implementation:**
- Recording hooks in Connection/Transaction (extend `lifecycle.rs`)
- Replay runtime with controlled scheduling (integrate with asupersync Lab reactor)
- Integrate with conflict tracer from bd-t6sv2.1 for conflict-point recording
- Build on existing `repro_fcw_loss.rs` and `deterministic_rebase.rs`
- Build on existing `time_travel.rs` for state inspection during replay

**Files to Modify:**
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs (recording hooks at transaction lifecycle points)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/core_types.rs (state capture serialization)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/repro_fcw_loss.rs (extend existing reproduction infrastructure)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/time_travel.rs (state inspection during replay)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (recording enable/disable PRAGMA)
- NEW: crates/fsqlite-replay/src/lib.rs (replay runtime and format)
- NEW: crates/fsqlite-replay/src/format.rs (binary recording format)
- NEW: crates/fsqlite-replay/src/minimizer.rs (delta debugging minimizer)

**CRITICAL: MVCC Preservation**
Replay MUST preserve concurrent writer semantics. Recordings capture the page-level MVCC state, not serialized execution. Replays must produce identical MVCC version chains. Recording overhead must be minimal when enabled and zero when disabled (feature gate).

**Test Requirements:**
- Unit: recording completeness for all operation types
  - Log: operation_type (begin|read|write|commit|rollback|savepoint), bytes_recorded, format_version
  - Verify: every operation type recorded with sufficient detail for replay
  - Verify: recording format is versioned and forward-compatible
  - Verify: recording can be deserialized after serialization (round-trip)
- Unit: replay determinism for single-transaction recordings
  - Log: recording_ops_count, replay_ops_count, state_checkpoints_matched, divergence_point
  - Verify: replay of single transaction produces identical final state
  - Verify: replay of deterministic operations is bitwise identical
- Unit: minimizer reduces test case size
  - Log: original_ops, minimized_ops, reduction_pct, failure_preserved
  - Verify: minimized case still reproduces the original failure
  - Verify: removing any further operation loses the failure (minimal)
- Integration: replay matches original for concurrent scenarios
  - Log: txn_count, conflict_count, replay_conflicts, state_match (true|false), divergence_location
  - Scenario: 4 concurrent writers with known conflicts -> record and replay
  - Verify: replay produces identical conflict outcomes
  - Verify: replay version chains match recording version chains
- Integration: diff analyzer identifies correct divergence point
  - Log: expected_state_hash, actual_state_hash, divergence_txn_id, divergence_page_id, explanation
  - Scenario: introduce intentional behavior difference -> verify diff identifies it
- E2E: real bugs reproducible from recordings
  - Log: bug_description, recording_size_bytes, replay_outcome, original_failure_reproduced
  - Verify: recording from one process replays correctly in another
  - Verify: recordings are self-contained (no external dependencies)
- E2E: recording overhead measurement
  - Log: throughput_without_recording, throughput_with_recording, overhead_pct, recording_bytes_per_op
  - Verify: recording overhead < 10% for typical workloads
  - Verify: zero overhead when recording disabled
- Proptest: arbitrary concurrent workloads replay correctly
  - Log: seed, txn_count, ops_per_txn, conflicts_detected, replay_match
  - Property: forall workloads, replay produces identical final database state

**Verification Script:**
Create scripts/verify_replay.sh that:
1. Runs a concurrent workload with recording enabled
2. Saves recording to file
3. Replays in a fresh database instance
4. Compares final state (page-level hash comparison)
5. Runs minimizer on the recording
6. Verifies minimized recording still reproduces same outcome
7. Generates detailed diff report on any mismatch

**Success Criteria:**
- "It happened once and I can't reproduce" eliminated for MVCC bugs
- Bug reports include reproducible recordings
- Regression tests auto-generated from production failure recordings
- Recordings are self-contained and portable across machines
- Builds on existing infrastructure (repro_fcw_loss.rs, time_travel.rs, Lab reactor)

 {"id":"bd-t6sv2.13","title":"Multi-Process Coordination Documentation & Testing","description":"**Idea #13 - Phase 3**

Document and test FrankenSQLite's multi-process concurrent writer behavior. Build on existing shared-memory infrastructure: `coordinator_ipc.rs` (83KB) and `shm.rs` (59KB).

**Components:**
1. **MPDocumentation**
   - Clear statement of MP support level (in-process MVCC fully supported, cross-process via shared memory)
   - Lock protocol documentation (page-level MVCC via shared memory segments)
   - Known limitations and workarounds
   - Architecture doc: how `coordinator_ipc.rs` manages cross-process MVCC coordination
   - Shared memory layout documentation (from `shm.rs`)

2. **MPTestSuite**
   - Fork-based concurrent access tests (multiple processes, same database)
   - Shared memory MVCC coordination verification
   - Crash-during-MP-write recovery testing
   - GC horizon advancement across processes (AtomicU64 in shared memory)
   - Cross-process SSI validation behavior

3. **MPCompatibility**
   - Comparison with C SQLite MP behavior (file-level locking vs MVCC)
   - Document behavioral differences (concurrent writers vs serialized)
   - Migration guide from MP C SQLite usage patterns
   - Document error code differences in MP scenarios

4. **MPMonitoring**
   - Process-level MVCC state visibility via shared memory
   - Cross-process conflict detection and reporting
   - Shared WAL diagnostics (which process holds which versions)
   - PRAGMA fsqlite_mp_stats: connected_processes, shared_mem_usage, cross_process_conflicts

**Implementation:**
- Documentation in docs/multi-process.md
- Test harness using std::process::Command for fork-based tests
- Integration with existing `coordinator_ipc.rs` and `shm.rs` infrastructure
- New PRAGMAs for MP visibility

**Files to Modify:**
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/coordinator_ipc.rs (MP monitoring hooks)
- crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/shm.rs (shared memory diagnostics)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/pragma.rs (new MP PRAGMAs)
- docs/multi-process.md (comprehensive documentation)

**CRITICAL: MVCC Preservation**
Multi-process documentation MUST emphasize that FrankenSQLite supports concurrent writers across processes (unlike C SQLite). Tests MUST verify that multiple processes can write concurrently without serialization. Never document single-process-only as a "recommended" configuration.

**Test Requirements:**
- Unit: shared memory layout correctness
  - Log: shm_size_bytes, gc_horizon_offset, page_lock_table_offset, process_count
  - Verify: shared memory structures properly aligned
  - Verify: atomic operations on shared memory are correct (SeqCst ordering)
- Unit: cross-process GC horizon advancement
  - Log: process_id, local_horizon, shared_horizon, advancement_delta
  - Verify: gc_horizon only advances when all processes agree
  - Verify: single slow process correctly pins the horizon
- Integration: two-process concurrent writes
  - Log: process_a_writes, process_b_writes, conflicts, total_committed, elapsed_ms
  - Scenario: two processes writing to different tables -> both commit concurrently
  - Scenario: two processes writing to same table, different rows -> both commit
  - Scenario: two processes writing to same page -> FCW applies correctly
  - Verify: MVCC semantics identical to in-process concurrent writes
- Integration: crash recovery with active MP writers
  - Log: process_killed, surviving_process_state, recovery_action, data_integrity
  - Scenario: kill one process mid-transaction -> other process continues correctly
  - Scenario: kill one process mid-commit -> WAL recovery correct for both
  - Verify: no data corruption after process crash
- Integration: MP monitoring accuracy
  - Log: connected_processes, shared_mem_usage_bytes, cross_process_conflicts, stale_processes
  - Verify: PRAGMA fsqlite_mp_stats reflects actual process state
  - Verify: disconnected process detected within timeout period
- E2E: stress test multi-process access
  - Log: process_count, total_transactions, conflicts, throughput_txn_per_sec, errors
  - Verify: 4+ processes can write concurrently to same database
  - Verify: no deadlocks, no data corruption, no stale locks
  - Verify: throughput scales with process count (up to contention limit)
- E2E: documentation examples executable
  - Log: example_name, processes_spawned, execution_result, output_matches_expected
  - Verify: each MP example in docs produces expected behavior

**Verification Script:**
Create scripts/verify_multi_process.sh that:
1. Spawns N worker processes writing to same database
2. Verifies all transactions committed correctly
3. Checks shared memory cleanup after process exit
4. Tests crash recovery with SIGKILL during writes
5. Validates documentation examples
6. Outputs pass/fail with per-process statistics

**Success Criteria:**
- MP behavior fully documented with architecture diagrams
- No undiscovered MP edge cases (comprehensive test coverage)
- Users know what to expect with clear behavioral documentation
- Cross-process MVCC verified as correct (same semantics as in-process)
